economic_notes: Nation holds 49% of Wasaya Group and 51% of Wasaya Partnership accounted for by modified equity method. Investment in AVID FN Motel One LP of $333,000 made during year. School construction project of $22.9M in progress. 4-unit housing project under CMHC loan of $1,052,000.
notes: Fiscal year 2021 statements signed off March 30, 2023 - significant delay in audit completion. Budget note references 'Hollow Water First Nation' which appears to be an error in the document. Consolidated entity includes Nu-Mah-Koos Non-Profit Building Development Corporation and Pe-Tay-Ka-Win Development Corporation and subsidiaries. Wasaya Group (49%) and Wasaya Partnership (51%) included via modified equity method using December 31, 2020 year-end. Large school construction project ($22.9M CIP) driving significant deferred revenue of $31.6M. CMHC revenue of $3,313,231 per income statement differs from segment total of $3,563,216 - possible rounding or allocation difference. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $40,491,975 + Health Canada $9,780,462 + CMHC $3,313,231 + Canada Post $578,229 + Provincial $692,684 - note Canada Post treated as government transfer. OSR calculated as other revenue $10,272,721 + Ontario First Nation LLP $1,442,032 + Local revenue $46,664 + SLAAMB $286,751 net of deferrals approximation; used segment 'Other' total of $12,490,866 as OSR.
